title: Sony AI wins at table tennis as silicon splits in two - TCR 04/23/26
description:
Sony AI's table tennis system Ace won three of five official matches against elite human players, Google split its eighth-generation TPU into separate training and inference chips, and North Korean hackers used commercial AI to vibe-code an entire malware campaign.
This episode covers the Ace *Nature* paper and what physical AI competition reveals about human capability, the $54 billion Pentagon autonomous warfare request, CISA's exclusion from Mythos, the HexagonalRodent malware campaign, Google's TPU 8t and 8i architecture, Cache Energy's thermal battery pilot, and a *Nature* study reframing hallucination as an incentive problem.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-23-2026/

0:00 Open and framing
0:41 Sony AI Ace beats elite table tennis players
4:01 Pentagon $54 billion autonomous warfare request
7:21 North Korean vibe-coded malware campaign
10:49 Google TPU 8t and 8i split architecture
13:37 Cache Energy thermal battery at UMN Morris
17:13 AI hallucination as incentive problem
21:46 Close

Sony AI published results in *Nature* showing its autonomous Ace system defeated elite table tennis players under full ITTF competition rules - nine cameras tracking ball position and spin within milliseconds, a control system trained entirely in simulation. Former Olympian Kinjiro Nakamura watched Ace execute a shot he called physically impossible, then recognized humans could learn the technique - a machine revealing unused capacity within the physics of a human body. North Korean operators used ChatGPT, Cursor, and Anima to build complete malware infrastructure without any prior coding skill, compromising 2,000 computers and stealing up to $12 million, while CISA remains excluded from Anthropic's Mythos Preview even as the NSA deploys it. The Pentagon requested $54 billion for autonomous drone warfare - a 24,000% year-over-year increase - as Anthropic filed its D.C. Circuit appellate brief arguing the supply-chain risk designation was retaliation. Google split its eighth-generation TPU into dedicated training and inference variants, replacing x86 hosts with ARM-based processors across both. Cache Energy installed a thermal battery in rural Minnesota that converts curtailed wind into 1,000°F heat stored in limestone, deployed in two hours. And a *Nature* study showed that accuracy benchmarks systematically reward confident guessing over admitting uncertainty - reframing hallucination as something the measurement instrument produces, fixable by changing the rubric rather than the model.
